attempt.claim: Deep open-core push (cross-model verified): the tetrad construction CANNOT be extended to size >=6 without new input (it is a one-seed Wilson-reflection machine, caps at the class {0,1,q,p-1-q,p-2}); #1056 reduces cleanly to the OPEN Hardy-Subbarao conjecture g(p)=#{n<p:n!≡-1}->infinity (since M(p)>=g(p)).
depends_on: OPEN: Hardy-Subbarao Problem C (g(p)->infinity) — a known open conjecture
no new unconditional construction; tetrad provably caps at 5; half-factorial sign ties to class-number parity (known/deep)
attempt.detail: Diagnosis of the size-5 cap: one odd seed q with q!≡1 gives only the reflected pair {q,p-1-q} plus {0,1,p-2} = size 5; a 6th hit needs an independent seed e!≡1 not forced by the mechanism (confirmed: p=53,q=37 has class exactly {0,1,15,37,51}). Size-6 classes DO exist (p=149 {0,1,25,98,123,147}; p=5039) but not via the tetrad. Half-factorial route: for p≡3 mod4, ((p-1)/2)!≡±1 with sign tied to class number h(-p) (Mordell/Cosgrave-Dilcher: ≡-1 iff h(-p)≡1 mod4) -> proving infinitely many favorable signs needs a new distribution theorem, no unconditional route. CLEAN CONDITIONAL: Hardy-Subbarao g(p)->infinity => sup_p M(p)=infinity => #1056 (all -1-hits lie in one class). sup_p g(p)=infinity already suffices for the full statement. All claims independently re-verified (factorial classes at p=53,149,5039).
attempt.insight: independent Opus verification of all factorial-residue classes (p=53,149,5039) and the M(p)>=g(p) bound.

provenance.proposer: GPT-5.5 Pro (open-core push), verified by Opus

attempt.reusable_for: Wilson reflection; factorial residue classes; Hardy-Subbarao g(p); one-seed-construction rigidity diagnosis
